title: lycopodium taxifolium var. passerinoides
intro: Lycopodium taxifolium var. passerinoides, commonly known as Passerina or Lycopodium passerinoides, is a variety of the Lycopodium taxifolium plant, which belongs to the club moss family (Lycopodiaceae). It is a type of evergreen, perennial plant that grows in shaded areas, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its resinous and astringent qualities. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and treat skin irritations. Lycopodium passerinoides can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and it is also used in homeopathy to stimulate the immune system and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
